title: papaver gorgoneum subsp. theresias
intro: Papaver gorgoneum subsp. theresias, a rare and lesser-known species of poppy, is a flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae, known for its striking red flowers with a distinctive black center. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and antispasmodic properties, derived from its alkaloid content, which includes thebaine and papaverine. The health benefits of Papaver gorgoneum subsp. theresias include pain relief, reduced inflammation, and relaxation of smooth muscle tissue, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as anxiety and insomnia. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are often used in homeopathic and naturopathic medicine.
